  36   
  37  # Regular expression for validating Visual Studio GUIDs.  If the GUID 
  38  # contains lowercase hex letters, MSVS will be fine. However, 
  39  # IncrediBuild BuildConsole will parse the solution file, but then 
  40  # silently skip building the target causing hard to track down errors. 
  41  # Note that this only happens with the BuildConsole, and does not occur 
  42  # if IncrediBuild is executed from inside Visual Studio.  This regex 
  43  # validates that the string looks like a GUID with all uppercase hex 
  44  # letters. 
  45  VALID_MSVS_GUID_CHARS = re.compile(r'^[A-F0-9\-]+$')

 102  cached_username = None 
 103

 105  cached_domain = None

 108  # TODO(gspencer): Switch the os.environ calls to be 
 109  # win32api.GetDomainName() and win32api.GetUserName() once the 
 110  # python version in depot_tools has been updated to work on Vista 
 111  # 64-bit. 

 112 -def _GetDomainAndUserName(): 

 113    if sys.platform not in ('win32', 'cygwin'): 
 114      return ('DOMAIN', 'USERNAME') 
 115    global cached_username 
 116    global cached_domain 
 117    if not cached_domain or not cached_username: 
 118      domain = os.environ.get('USERDOMAIN') 
 119      username = os.environ.get('USERNAME') 
 120      if not domain or not username: 
 121        call = subprocess.Popen(['net', 'config', 'Workstation'], 
 122                                stdout=subprocess.PIPE) 
 123        config = call.communicate()[0] 
 124        username_re = re.compile(r'^User name\s+(\S+)', re.MULTILINE) 
 125        username_match = username_re.search(config) 
 126        if username_match: 
 127          username = username_match.group(1) 
 128        domain_re = re.compile(r'^Logon domain\s+(\S+)', re.MULTILINE) 
 129        domain_match = domain_re.search(config) 
 130        if domain_match: 
 131          domain = domain_match.group(1) 
 132      cached_domain = domain 
 133      cached_username = username 
 134    return (cached_domain, cached_username)

 136  fixpath_prefix = None

 139 -def _NormalizedSource(source): 

 140    """Normalize the path. 
 141   
 142    But not if that gets rid of a variable, as this may expand to something 
 143    larger than one directory. 
 144   
 145    Arguments: 
 146        source: The path to be normalize.d 
 147   
 148    Returns: 
 149        The normalized path. 
 150    """ 
 151    normalized = os.path.normpath(source) 
 152    if source.count('$') == normalized.count('$'): 
 153      source = normalized 
 154    return source 

 155   
 156   

 157 -def _FixPath(path): 

 158    """Convert paths to a form that will make sense in a vcproj file. 
 159   
 160    Arguments: 
 161      path: The path to convert, may contain / etc. 
 162    Returns: 
 163      The path with all slashes made into backslashes. 
 164    """ 
 165    if fixpath_prefix and path and not os.path.isabs(path) and not path[0] == '$': 
 166      path = os.path.join(fixpath_prefix, path) 
 167    path = path.replace('/', '\\') 
 168    path = _NormalizedSource(path) 
 169    if path and path[-1] == '\\': 
 170      path = path[:-1] 
 171    return path 

 172   
 173   

 174 -def _FixPaths(paths): 

 175    """Fix each of the paths of the list.""" 
 176    return [_FixPath(i) for i in paths]

 179 -def _ConvertSourcesToFilterHierarchy(sources, prefix=None, excluded=None, 
 180                                       list_excluded=True, msvs_version=None): 

 181    """Converts a list split source file paths into a vcproj folder hierarchy. 
 182   
 183    Arguments: 
 184      sources: A list of source file paths split. 
 185      prefix: A list of source file path layers meant to apply to each of sources. 
 186      excluded: A set of excluded files. 
 187      msvs_version: A MSVSVersion object. 
 188   
 189    Returns: 
 190      A hierarchy of filenames and MSVSProject.Filter objects that matches the 
 191      layout of the source tree. 
 192      For example: 
 193      _ConvertSourcesToFilterHierarchy([['a', 'bob1.c'], ['b', 'bob2.c']], 
 194                                       prefix=['joe']) 
 195      --> 
 196      [MSVSProject.Filter('a', contents=['joe\\a\\bob1.c']), 
 197       MSVSProject.Filter('b', contents=['joe\\b\\bob2.c'])] 
 198    """ 
 199    if not prefix: prefix = [] 
 200    result = [] 
 201    excluded_result = [] 
 202    folders = OrderedDict() 
 203    # Gather files into the final result, excluded, or folders. 
 204    for s in sources: 
 205      if len(s) == 1: 
 206        filename = _NormalizedSource('\\'.join(prefix + s)) 
 207        if filename in excluded: 
 208          excluded_result.append(filename) 
 209        else: 
 210          result.append(filename) 
 211      elif msvs_version and not msvs_version.UsesVcxproj(): 
 212        # For MSVS 2008 and earlier, we need to process all files before walking 
 213        # the sub folders. 
 214        if not folders.get(s[0]): 
 215          folders[s[0]] = [] 
 216        folders[s[0]].append(s[1:]) 
 217      else: 
 218        contents = _ConvertSourcesToFilterHierarchy([s[1:]], prefix + [s[0]], 
 219                                                    excluded=excluded, 
 220                                                    list_excluded=list_excluded, 
 221                                                    msvs_version=msvs_version) 
 222        contents = MSVSProject.Filter(s[0], contents=contents) 
 223        result.append(contents) 
 224    # Add a folder for excluded files. 
 225    if excluded_result and list_excluded: 
 226      excluded_folder = MSVSProject.Filter('_excluded_files', 
 227                                           contents=excluded_result) 
 228      result.append(excluded_folder) 
 229   
 230    if msvs_version and msvs_version.UsesVcxproj(): 
 231      return result 
 232   
 233    # Populate all the folders. 
 234    for f in folders: 
 235      contents = _ConvertSourcesToFilterHierarchy(folders[f], prefix=prefix + [f], 
 236                                                  excluded=excluded, 
 237                                                  list_excluded=list_excluded, 
 238                                                  msvs_version=msvs_version) 
 239      contents = MSVSProject.Filter(f, contents=contents) 
 240      result.append(contents) 
 241    return result 

 242

 244 -def _ToolAppend(tools, tool_name, setting, value, only_if_unset=False): 

 245    if not value: return 
 246    _ToolSetOrAppend(tools, tool_name, setting, value, only_if_unset)

 248   

 249 -def _ToolSetOrAppend(tools, tool_name, setting, value, only_if_unset=False): 

 250    # TODO(bradnelson): ugly hack, fix this more generally!!! 
 251    if 'Directories' in setting or 'Dependencies' in setting: 
 252      if type(value) == str: 
 253        value = value.replace('/', '\\') 
 254      else: 
 255        value = [i.replace('/', '\\') for i in value] 
 256    if not tools.get(tool_name): 
 257      tools[tool_name] = dict() 
 258    tool = tools[tool_name] 
 259    if tool.get(setting): 
 260      if only_if_unset: return 
 261      if type(tool[setting]) == list and type(value) == list: 
 262        tool[setting] += value 
 263      else: 
 264        raise TypeError( 
 265            'Appending "%s" to a non-list setting "%s" for tool "%s" is ' 
 266            'not allowed, previous value: %s' % ( 
 267                value, setting, tool_name, str(tool[setting]))) 
 268    else: 
 269      tool[setting] = value

 276 -def _ConfigBaseName(config_name, platform_name): 

 277    if config_name.endswith('_' + platform_name): 
 278      return config_name[0:-len(platform_name) - 1]

 280      return config_name

 283 -def _ConfigFullName(config_name, config_data): 

 284    platform_name = _ConfigPlatform(config_data) 
 285    return '%s|%s' % (_ConfigBaseName(config_name, platform_name), platform_name)

 288 -def _ConfigWindowsTargetPlatformVersion(config_data): 

 289    ver = config_data.get('msvs_windows_target_platform_version') 
 290    if not ver or re.match(r'^\d+', ver): 
 291      return ver 
 292    for key in [r'HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Microsoft SDKs\Windows\%s', 
 293                r'HKLM\Software\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\Microsoft SDKs\Windows\%s']: 
 294      sdkdir = MSVSVersion._RegistryGetValue(key % ver, 'InstallationFolder') 
 295      if not sdkdir: 
 296        continue 
 297      version = MSVSVersion._RegistryGetValue(key % ver, 'ProductVersion') or '' 
 298      # find a matching entry in sdkdir\include 
 299      names = sorted([x for x in os.listdir(r'%s\include' % sdkdir) \ 
 300                      if x.startswith(version)], reverse = True) 
 301      return names[0]

 304 -def _BuildCommandLineForRuleRaw(spec, cmd, cygwin_shell, has_input_path, 
 305                                  quote_cmd, do_setup_env): 

 306   
 307    if [x for x in cmd if '$(InputDir)' in x]: 
 308      input_dir_preamble = ( 
 309        'set INPUTDIR=$(InputDir)\n' 
 310        'if NOT DEFINED INPUTDIR set INPUTDIR=.\\\n' 
 311        'set INPUTDIR=%INPUTDIR:~0,-1%\n' 
 312        ) 
 313    else: 
 314      input_dir_preamble = '' 
 315   
 316    if cygwin_shell: 
 317      # Find path to cygwin. 
 318      cygwin_dir = _FixPath(spec.get('msvs_cygwin_dirs', ['.'])[0]) 
 319      # Prepare command. 
 320      direct_cmd = cmd 
 321      direct_cmd = [i.replace('$(IntDir)', 
 322                              '`cygpath -m "${INTDIR}"`') for i in direct_cmd] 
 323      direct_cmd = [i.replace('$(OutDir)', 
 324                              '`cygpath -m "${OUTDIR}"`') for i in direct_cmd] 
 325      direct_cmd = [i.replace('$(InputDir)', 
 326                              '`cygpath -m "${INPUTDIR}"`') for i in direct_cmd] 
 327      if has_input_path: 
 328        direct_cmd = [i.replace('$(InputPath)', 
 329                                '`cygpath -m "${INPUTPATH}"`') 
 330                      for i in direct_cmd] 
 331      direct_cmd = ['\\"%s\\"' % i.replace('"', '\\\\\\"') for i in direct_cmd] 
 332      # direct_cmd = gyp.common.EncodePOSIXShellList(direct_cmd) 
 333      direct_cmd = ' '.join(direct_cmd) 
 334      # TODO(quote):  regularize quoting path names throughout the module 
 335      cmd = '' 
 336      if do_setup_env: 
 337        cmd += 'call "$(ProjectDir)%(cygwin_dir)s\\setup_env.bat" && ' 
 338      cmd += 'set CYGWIN=nontsec&& ' 
 339      if direct_cmd.find('NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS') >= 0: 
 340        cmd += 'set /a NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS_PLUS_1=%%NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS%%+1&& ' 
 341      if direct_cmd.find('INTDIR') >= 0: 
 342        cmd += 'set INTDIR=$(IntDir)&& ' 
 343      if direct_cmd.find('OUTDIR') >= 0: 
 344        cmd += 'set OUTDIR=$(OutDir)&& ' 
 345      if has_input_path and direct_cmd.find('INPUTPATH') >= 0: 
 346        cmd += 'set INPUTPATH=$(InputPath) && ' 
 347      cmd += 'bash -c "%(cmd)s"' 
 348      cmd = cmd % {'cygwin_dir': cygwin_dir, 
 349                   'cmd': direct_cmd} 
 350      return input_dir_preamble + cmd 
 351    else: 
 352      # Convert cat --> type to mimic unix. 
 353      if cmd[0] == 'cat': 
 354        command = ['type'] 
 355      else: 
 356        command = [cmd[0].replace('/', '\\')] 
 357      # Add call before command to ensure that commands can be tied together one 
 358      # after the other without aborting in Incredibuild, since IB makes a bat 
 359      # file out of the raw command string, and some commands (like python) are 
 360      # actually batch files themselves. 
 361      command.insert(0, 'call') 
 362      # Fix the paths 
 363      # TODO(quote): This is a really ugly heuristic, and will miss path fixing 
 364      #              for arguments like "--arg=path" or "/opt:path". 
 365      # If the argument starts with a slash or dash, it's probably a command line 
 366      # switch 
 367      arguments = [i if (i[:1] in "/-") else _FixPath(i) for i in cmd[1:]] 
 368      arguments = [i.replace('$(InputDir)', '%INPUTDIR%') for i in arguments] 
 369      arguments = [MSVSSettings.FixVCMacroSlashes(i) for i in arguments] 
 370      if quote_cmd: 
 371        # Support a mode for using cmd directly. 
 372        # Convert any paths to native form (first element is used directly). 
 373        # TODO(quote):  regularize quoting path names throughout the module 
 374        arguments = ['"%s"' % i for i in arguments] 
 375      # Collapse into a single command. 
 376      return input_dir_preamble + ' '.join(command + arguments) 

 377   
 378   

 379 -def _BuildCommandLineForRule(spec, rule, has_input_path, do_setup_env): 

 380    # Currently this weird argument munging is used to duplicate the way a 
 381    # python script would need to be run as part of the chrome tree. 
 382    # Eventually we should add some sort of rule_default option to set this 
 383    # per project. For now the behavior chrome needs is the default. 
 384    mcs = rule.get('msvs_cygwin_shell') 
 385    if mcs is None: 
 386      mcs = int(spec.get('msvs_cygwin_shell', 1)) 
 387    elif isinstance(mcs, str): 
 388      mcs = int(mcs) 
 389    quote_cmd = int(rule.get('msvs_quote_cmd', 1)) 
 390    return _BuildCommandLineForRuleRaw(spec, rule['action'], mcs, has_input_path, 
 391                                       quote_cmd, do_setup_env=do_setup_env) 

 392

 394 -def _AddActionStep(actions_dict, inputs, outputs, description, command): 

 395    """Merge action into an existing list of actions. 
 396   
 397    Care must be taken so that actions which have overlapping inputs either don't 
 398    get assigned to the same input, or get collapsed into one. 
 399   
 400    Arguments: 
 401      actions_dict: dictionary keyed on input name, which maps to a list of 
 402        dicts describing the actions attached to that input file. 
 403      inputs: list of inputs 
 404      outputs: list of outputs 
 405      description: description of the action 
 406      command: command line to execute 
 407    """ 
 408    # Require there to be at least one input (call sites will ensure this). 
 409    assert inputs 
 410   
 411    action = { 
 412        'inputs': inputs, 
 413        'outputs': outputs, 
 414        'description': description, 
 415        'command': command, 
 416    } 
 417   
 418    # Pick where to stick this action. 
 419    # While less than optimal in terms of build time, attach them to the first 
 420    # input for now. 
 421    chosen_input = inputs[0] 
 422   
 423    # Add it there. 
 424    if chosen_input not in actions_dict: 
 425      actions_dict[chosen_input] = [] 
 426    actions_dict[chosen_input].append(action) 

 427   
 428   

 429 -def _AddCustomBuildToolForMSVS(p, spec, primary_input, 
 430                                 inputs, outputs, description, cmd): 

 431    """Add a custom build tool to execute something. 
 432   
 433    Arguments: 
 434      p: the target project 
 435      spec: the target project dict 
 436      primary_input: input file to attach the build tool to 
 437      inputs: list of inputs 
 438      outputs: list of outputs 
 439      description: description of the action 
 440      cmd: command line to execute 
 441    """ 
 442    inputs = _FixPaths(inputs) 
 443    outputs = _FixPaths(outputs) 
 444    tool = MSVSProject.Tool( 
 445        'VCCustomBuildTool', 
 446        {'Description': description, 
 447         'AdditionalDependencies': ';'.join(inputs), 
 448         'Outputs': ';'.join(outputs), 
 449         'CommandLine': cmd, 
 450        }) 
 451    # Add to the properties of primary input for each config. 
 452    for config_name, c_data in spec['configurations'].iteritems(): 
 453      p.AddFileConfig(_FixPath(primary_input), 
 454                      _ConfigFullName(config_name, c_data), tools=[tool]) 

 455   
 456   

 457 -def _AddAccumulatedActionsToMSVS(p, spec, actions_dict): 

 458    """Add actions accumulated into an actions_dict, merging as needed. 
 459   
 460    Arguments: 
 461      p: the target project 
 462      spec: the target project dict 
 463      actions_dict: dictionary keyed on input name, which maps to a list of 
 464          dicts describing the actions attached to that input file. 
 465    """ 
 466    for primary_input in actions_dict: 
 467      inputs = OrderedSet() 
 468      outputs = OrderedSet() 
 469      descriptions = [] 
 470      commands = [] 
 471      for action in actions_dict[primary_input]: 
 472        inputs.update(OrderedSet(action['inputs'])) 
 473        outputs.update(OrderedSet(action['outputs'])) 
 474        descriptions.append(action['description']) 
 475        commands.append(action['command']) 
 476      # Add the custom build step for one input file. 
 477      description = ', and also '.join(descriptions) 
 478      command = '\r\n'.join(commands) 
 479      _AddCustomBuildToolForMSVS(p, spec, 
 480                                 primary_input=primary_input, 
 481                                 inputs=inputs, 
 482                                 outputs=outputs, 
 483                                 description=description, 
 484                                 cmd=command) 

 485   
 486   

 487 -def _RuleExpandPath(path, input_file): 

 488    """Given the input file to which a rule applied, string substitute a path. 
 489   
 490    Arguments: 
 491      path: a path to string expand 
 492      input_file: the file to which the rule applied. 
 493    Returns: 
 494      The string substituted path. 
 495    """ 
 496    path = path.replace('$(InputName)', 
 497                        os.path.splitext(os.path.split(input_file)[1])[0]) 
 498    path = path.replace('$(InputDir)', os.path.dirname(input_file)) 
 499    path = path.replace('$(InputExt)', 
 500                        os.path.splitext(os.path.split(input_file)[1])[1]) 
 501    path = path.replace('$(InputFileName)', os.path.split(input_file)[1]) 
 502    path = path.replace('$(InputPath)', input_file) 
 503    return path 

 504   
 505   

 506 -def _FindRuleTriggerFiles(rule, sources): 

 507    """Find the list of files which a particular rule applies to. 
 508   
 509    Arguments: 
 510      rule: the rule in question 
 511      sources: the set of all known source files for this project 
 512    Returns: 
 513      The list of sources that trigger a particular rule. 
 514    """ 
 515    return rule.get('rule_sources', []) 

 516   
 517   

 518 -def _RuleInputsAndOutputs(rule, trigger_file): 

 519    """Find the inputs and outputs generated by a rule. 
 520   
 521    Arguments: 
 522      rule: the rule in question. 
 523      trigger_file: the main trigger for this rule. 
 524    Returns: 
 525      The pair of (inputs, outputs) involved in this rule. 
 526    """ 
 527    raw_inputs = _FixPaths(rule.get('inputs', [])) 
 528    raw_outputs = _FixPaths(rule.get('outputs', [])) 
 529    inputs = OrderedSet() 
 530    outputs = OrderedSet() 
 531    inputs.add(trigger_file) 
 532    for i in raw_inputs: 
 533      inputs.add(_RuleExpandPath(i, trigger_file)) 
 534    for o in raw_outputs: 
 535      outputs.add(_RuleExpandPath(o, trigger_file)) 
 536    return (inputs, outputs) 

 537   
 538   

 539 -def _GenerateNativeRulesForMSVS(p, rules, output_dir, spec, options): 

 540    """Generate a native rules file. 
 541   
 542    Arguments: 
 543      p: the target project 
 544      rules: the set of rules to include 
 545      output_dir: the directory in which the project/gyp resides 
 546      spec: the project dict 
 547      options: global generator options 
 548    """ 
 549    rules_filename = '%s%s.rules' % (spec['target_name'], 
 550                                     options.suffix) 
 551    rules_file = MSVSToolFile.Writer(os.path.join(output_dir, rules_filename), 
 552                                     spec['target_name']) 
 553    # Add each rule. 
 554    for r in rules: 
 555      rule_name = r['rule_name'] 
 556      rule_ext = r['extension'] 
 557      inputs = _FixPaths(r.get('inputs', [])) 
 558      outputs = _FixPaths(r.get('outputs', [])) 
 559      # Skip a rule with no action and no inputs. 
 560      if 'action' not in r and not r.get('rule_sources', []): 
 561        continue 
 562      cmd = _BuildCommandLineForRule(spec, r, has_input_path=True, 
 563                                     do_setup_env=True) 
 564      rules_file.AddCustomBuildRule(name=rule_name, 
 565                                    description=r.get('message', rule_name), 
 566                                    extensions=[rule_ext], 
 567                                    additional_dependencies=inputs, 
 568                                    outputs=outputs, 
 569                                    cmd=cmd) 
 570    # Write out rules file. 
 571    rules_file.WriteIfChanged() 
 572   
 573    # Add rules file to project. 
 574    p.AddToolFile(rules_filename) 

 575   
 576   

 577 -def _Cygwinify(path): 

 578    path = path.replace('$(OutDir)', '$(OutDirCygwin)') 
 579    path = path.replace('$(IntDir)', '$(IntDirCygwin)') 
 580    return path 

 581   
 582   

 583 -def _GenerateExternalRules(rules, output_dir, spec, 
 584                             sources, options, actions_to_add): 

 585    """Generate an external makefile to do a set of rules. 
 586   
 587    Arguments: 
 588      rules: the list of rules to include 
 589      output_dir: path containing project and gyp files 
 590      spec: project specification data 
 591      sources: set of sources known 
 592      options: global generator options 
 593      actions_to_add: The list of actions we will add to. 
 594    """ 
 595    filename = '%s_rules%s.mk' % (spec['target_name'], options.suffix) 
 596    mk_file = gyp.common.WriteOnDiff(os.path.join(output_dir, filename)) 
 597    # Find cygwin style versions of some paths. 
 598    mk_file.write('OutDirCygwin:=$(shell cygpath -u "$(OutDir)")\n') 
 599    mk_file.write('IntDirCygwin:=$(shell cygpath -u "$(IntDir)")\n') 
 600    # Gather stuff needed to emit all: target. 
 601    all_inputs = OrderedSet() 
 602    all_outputs = OrderedSet() 
 603    all_output_dirs = OrderedSet() 
 604    first_outputs = [] 
 605    for rule in rules: 
 606      trigger_files = _FindRuleTriggerFiles(rule, sources) 
 607      for tf in trigger_files: 
 608        inputs, outputs = _RuleInputsAndOutputs(rule, tf) 
 609        all_inputs.update(OrderedSet(inputs)) 
 610        all_outputs.update(OrderedSet(outputs)) 
 611        # Only use one target from each rule as the dependency for 
 612        # 'all' so we don't try to build each rule multiple times. 
 613        first_outputs.append(list(outputs)[0]) 
 614        # Get the unique output directories for this rule. 
 615        output_dirs = [os.path.split(i)[0] for i in outputs] 
 616        for od in output_dirs: 
 617          all_output_dirs.add(od) 
 618    first_outputs_cyg = [_Cygwinify(i) for i in first_outputs] 
 619    # Write out all: target, including mkdir for each output directory. 
 620    mk_file.write('all: %s\n' % ' '.join(first_outputs_cyg)) 
 621    for od in all_output_dirs: 
 622      if od: 
 623        mk_file.write('\tmkdir -p `cygpath -u "%s"`\n' % od) 
 624    mk_file.write('\n') 
 625    # Define how each output is generated. 
 626    for rule in rules: 
 627      trigger_files = _FindRuleTriggerFiles(rule, sources) 
 628      for tf in trigger_files: 
 629        # Get all the inputs and outputs for this rule for this trigger file. 
 630        inputs, outputs = _RuleInputsAndOutputs(rule, tf) 
 631        inputs = [_Cygwinify(i) for i in inputs] 
 632        outputs = [_Cygwinify(i) for i in outputs] 
 633        # Prepare the command line for this rule. 
 634        cmd = [_RuleExpandPath(c, tf) for c in rule['action']] 
 635        cmd = ['"%s"' % i for i in cmd] 
 636        cmd = ' '.join(cmd) 
 637        # Add it to the makefile. 
 638        mk_file.write('%s: %s\n' % (' '.join(outputs), ' '.join(inputs))) 
 639        mk_file.write('\t%s\n\n' % cmd) 
 640    # Close up the file. 
 641    mk_file.close() 
 642   
 643    # Add makefile to list of sources. 
 644    sources.add(filename) 
 645    # Add a build action to call makefile. 
 646    cmd = ['make', 
 647           'OutDir=$(OutDir)', 
 648           'IntDir=$(IntDir)', 
 649           '-j', '${NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS_PLUS_1}', 
 650           '-f', filename] 
 651    cmd = _BuildCommandLineForRuleRaw(spec, cmd, True, False, True, True) 
 652    # Insert makefile as 0'th input, so it gets the action attached there, 
 653    # as this is easier to understand from in the IDE. 
 654    all_inputs = list(all_inputs) 
 655    all_inputs.insert(0, filename) 
 656    _AddActionStep(actions_to_add, 
 657                   inputs=_FixPaths(all_inputs), 
 658                   outputs=_FixPaths(all_outputs), 
 659                   description='Running external rules for %s' % 
 660                       spec['target_name'], 
 661                   command=cmd) 

 662   
 663   

 664 -def _EscapeEnvironmentVariableExpansion(s): 

 665    """Escapes % characters. 
 666   
 667    Escapes any % characters so that Windows-style environment variable 
 668    expansions will leave them alone. 
 669    See http://connect.microsoft.com/VisualStudio/feedback/details/106127/cl-d-name-text-containing-percentage-characters-doesnt-compile 
 670    to understand why we have to do this. 
 671   
 672    Args: 
 673        s: The string to be escaped. 
 674   
 675    Returns: 
 676        The escaped string. 
 677    """ 
 678    s = s.replace('%', '%%') 
 679    return s 

 680   
 681   
 682  quote_replacer_regex = re.compile(r'(\\*)"') 
 683   
 684   

 685 -def _EscapeCommandLineArgumentForMSVS(s): 

 686    """Escapes a Windows command-line argument. 
 687   
 688    So that the Win32 CommandLineToArgv function will turn the escaped result back 
 689    into the original string. 
 690    See http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/17w5ykft.aspx 
 691    ("Parsing C++ Command-Line Arguments") to understand why we have to do 
 692    this. 
 693   
 694    Args: 
 695        s: the string to be escaped. 
 696    Returns: 
 697        the escaped string. 
 698    """ 
 699   
 700    def _Replace(match): 
 701      # For a literal quote, CommandLineToArgv requires an odd number of 
 702      # backslashes preceding it, and it produces half as many literal backslashes 
 703      # (rounded down). So we need to produce 2n+1 backslashes. 
 704      return 2 * match.group(1) + '\\"' 

 705   
 706    # Escape all quotes so that they are interpreted literally. 
 707    s = quote_replacer_regex.sub(_Replace, s) 
 708    # Now add unescaped quotes so that any whitespace is interpreted literally. 
 709    s = '"' + s + '"' 
 710    return s 
 711   
 712   
 713  delimiters_replacer_regex = re.compile(r'(\\*)([,;]+)') 
 714   
 715   

 716 -def _EscapeVCProjCommandLineArgListItem(s): 

 717    """Escapes command line arguments for MSVS. 
 718   
 719    The VCProj format stores string lists in a single string using commas and 
 720    semi-colons as separators, which must be quoted if they are to be 
 721    interpreted literally. However, command-line arguments may already have 
 722    quotes, and the VCProj parser is ignorant of the backslash escaping 
 723    convention used by CommandLineToArgv, so the command-line quotes and the 
 724    VCProj quotes may not be the same quotes. So to store a general 
 725    command-line argument in a VCProj list, we need to parse the existing 
 726    quoting according to VCProj's convention and quote any delimiters that are 
 727    not already quoted by that convention. The quotes that we add will also be 
 728    seen by CommandLineToArgv, so if backslashes precede them then we also have 
 729    to escape those backslashes according to the CommandLineToArgv 
 730    convention. 
 731   
 732    Args: 
 733        s: the string to be escaped. 
 734    Returns: 
 735        the escaped string. 
 736    """ 
 737   
 738    def _Replace(match): 
 739      # For a non-literal quote, CommandLineToArgv requires an even number of 
 740      # backslashes preceding it, and it produces half as many literal 
 741      # backslashes. So we need to produce 2n backslashes. 
 742      return 2 * match.group(1) + '"' + match.group(2) + '"' 

 743   
 744    segments = s.split('"') 
 745    # The unquoted segments are at the even-numbered indices. 
 746    for i in range(0, len(segments), 2): 
 747      segments[i] = delimiters_replacer_regex.sub(_Replace, segments[i]) 
 748    # Concatenate back into a single string 
 749    s = '"'.join(segments) 
 750    if len(segments) % 2 == 0: 
 751      # String ends while still quoted according to VCProj's convention. This 
 752      # means the delimiter and the next list item that follow this one in the 
 753      # .vcproj file will be misinterpreted as part of this item. There is nothing 
 754      # we can do about this. Adding an extra quote would correct the problem in 
 755      # the VCProj but cause the same problem on the final command-line. Moving 
 756      # the item to the end of the list does works, but that's only possible if 
 757      # there's only one such item. Let's just warn the user. 
 758      print >> sys.stderr, ('Warning: MSVS may misinterpret the odd number of ' + 
 759                            'quotes in ' + s) 
 760    return s 
 761   
 762   

 763 -def _EscapeCppDefineForMSVS(s): 

 764    """Escapes a CPP define so that it will reach the compiler unaltered.""" 
 765    s = _EscapeEnvironmentVariableExpansion(s) 
 766    s = _EscapeCommandLineArgumentForMSVS(s) 
 767    s = _EscapeVCProjCommandLineArgListItem(s) 
 768    # cl.exe replaces literal # characters with = in preprocesor definitions for 
 769    # some reason. Octal-encode to work around that. 
 770    s = s.replace('#', '\\%03o' % ord('#')) 
 771    return s 

 772   
 773   
 774  quote_replacer_regex2 = re.compile(r'(\\+)"') 
 775   
 776   

 777 -def _EscapeCommandLineArgumentForMSBuild(s): 

 778    """Escapes a Windows command-line argument for use by MSBuild.""" 
 779   
 780    def _Replace(match): 
 781      return (len(match.group(1)) / 2 * 4) * '\\' + '\\"' 

 782   
 783    # Escape all quotes so that they are interpreted literally. 
 784    s = quote_replacer_regex2.sub(_Replace, s) 
 785    return s 
 786   
 787   

 788 -def _EscapeMSBuildSpecialCharacters(s): 

 789    escape_dictionary = { 
 790        '%': '%25', 
 791        '$': '%24', 
 792        '@': '%40', 
 793        "'": '%27', 
 794        ';': '%3B', 
 795        '?': '%3F', 
 796        '*': '%2A' 
 797        } 
 798    result = ''.join([escape_dictionary.get(c, c) for c in s]) 
 799    return result 

 800   
 801   

 802 -def _EscapeCppDefineForMSBuild(s): 

 803    """Escapes a CPP define so that it will reach the compiler unaltered.""" 
 804    s = _EscapeEnvironmentVariableExpansion(s) 
 805    s = _EscapeCommandLineArgumentForMSBuild(s) 
 806    s = _EscapeMSBuildSpecialCharacters(s) 
 807    # cl.exe replaces literal # characters with = in preprocesor definitions for 
 808    # some reason. Octal-encode to work around that. 
 809    s = s.replace('#', '\\%03o' % ord('#')) 
 810    return s 

 811   
 812   

 813 -def _GenerateRulesForMSVS(p, output_dir, options, spec, 
 814                            sources, excluded_sources, 
 815                            actions_to_add): 

 816    """Generate all the rules for a particular project. 
 817   
 818    Arguments: 
 819      p: the project 
 820      output_dir: directory to emit rules to 
 821      options: global options passed to the generator 
 822      spec: the specification for this project 
 823      sources: the set of all known source files in this project 
 824      excluded_sources: the set of sources excluded from normal processing 
 825      actions_to_add: deferred list of actions to add in 
 826    """ 
 827    rules = spec.get('rules', []) 
 828    rules_native = [r for r in rules if not int(r.get('msvs_external_rule', 0))] 
 829    rules_external = [r for r in rules if int(r.get('msvs_external_rule', 0))] 
 830   
 831    # Handle rules that use a native rules file. 
 832    if rules_native: 
 833      _GenerateNativeRulesForMSVS(p, rules_native, output_dir, spec, options) 
 834   
 835    # Handle external rules (non-native rules). 
 836    if rules_external: 
 837      _GenerateExternalRules(rules_external, output_dir, spec, 
 838                             sources, options, actions_to_add) 
 839    _AdjustSourcesForRules(rules, sources, excluded_sources, False) 

 840   
 841   

 842 -def _AdjustSourcesForRules(rules, sources, excluded_sources, is_msbuild): 

 843    # Add outputs generated by each rule (if applicable). 
 844    for rule in rules: 
 845      # Add in the outputs from this rule. 
 846      trigger_files = _FindRuleTriggerFiles(rule, sources) 
 847      for trigger_file in trigger_files: 
 848        # Remove trigger_file from excluded_sources to let the rule be triggered 
 849        # (e.g. rule trigger ax_enums.idl is added to excluded_sources 
 850        # because it's also in an action's inputs in the same project) 
 851        excluded_sources.discard(_FixPath(trigger_file)) 
 852        # Done if not processing outputs as sources. 
 853        if int(rule.get('process_outputs_as_sources', False)): 
 854          inputs, outputs = _RuleInputsAndOutputs(rule, trigger_file) 
 855          inputs = OrderedSet(_FixPaths(inputs)) 
 856          outputs = OrderedSet(_FixPaths(outputs)) 
 857          inputs.remove(_FixPath(trigger_file)) 
 858          sources.update(inputs) 
 859          if not is_msbuild: 
 860            excluded_sources.update(inputs) 
 861          sources.update(outputs) 

 862   
 863   

 864 -def _FilterActionsFromExcluded(excluded_sources, actions_to_add): 

 865    """Take inputs with actions attached out of the list of exclusions. 
 866   
 867    Arguments: 
 868      excluded_sources: list of source files not to be built. 
 869      actions_to_add: dict of actions keyed on source file they're attached to. 
 870    Returns: 
 871      excluded_sources with files that have actions attached removed. 
 872    """ 
 873    must_keep = OrderedSet(_FixPaths(actions_to_add.keys())) 
 874    return [s for s in excluded_sources if s not in must_keep] 

 875   
 876   

 877 -def _GetDefaultConfiguration(spec): 

 878    return spec['configurations'][spec['default_configuration']] 

 879   
 880   

 881 -def _GetGuidOfProject(proj_path, spec): 

 882    """Get the guid for the project. 
 883   
 884    Arguments: 
 885      proj_path: Path of the vcproj or vcxproj file to generate. 
 886      spec: The target dictionary containing the properties of the target. 
 887    Returns: 
 888      the guid. 
 889    Raises: 
 890      ValueError: if the specified GUID is invalid. 
 891    """ 
 892    # Pluck out the default configuration. 
 893    default_config = _GetDefaultConfiguration(spec) 
 894    # Decide the guid of the project. 
 895    guid = default_config.get('msvs_guid') 
 896    if guid: 
 897      if VALID_MSVS_GUID_CHARS.match(guid) is None: 
 898        raise ValueError('Invalid MSVS guid: "%s".  Must match regex: "%s".' % 
 899                         (guid, VALID_MSVS_GUID_CHARS.pattern)) 
 900      guid = '{%s}' % guid 
 901    guid = guid or MSVSNew.MakeGuid(proj_path) 
 902    return guid 

 903   
 904   

 905 -def _GetMsbuildToolsetOfProject(proj_path, spec, version): 

 906    """Get the platform toolset for the project. 
 907   
 908    Arguments: 
 909      proj_path: Path of the vcproj or vcxproj file to generate. 
 910      spec: The target dictionary containing the properties of the target. 
 911      version: The MSVSVersion object. 
 912    Returns: 
 913      the platform toolset string or None. 
 914    """ 
 915    # Pluck out the default configuration. 
 916    default_config = _GetDefaultConfiguration(spec) 
 917    toolset = default_config.get('msbuild_toolset') 
 918    if not toolset and version.DefaultToolset(): 
 919      toolset = version.DefaultToolset() 
 920    return toolset 

 921   
 922   

 923 -def _GenerateProject(project, options, version, generator_flags): 

 924    """Generates a vcproj file. 
 925   
 926    Arguments: 
 927      project: the MSVSProject object. 
 928      options: global generator options. 
 929      version: the MSVSVersion object. 
 930      generator_flags: dict of generator-specific flags. 
 931    Returns: 
 932      A list of source files that cannot be found on disk. 
 933    """ 
 934    default_config = _GetDefaultConfiguration(project.spec) 
 935   
 936    # Skip emitting anything if told to with msvs_existing_vcproj option. 
 937    if default_config.get('msvs_existing_vcproj'): 
 938      return [] 
 939   
 940    if version.UsesVcxproj(): 
 941      return _GenerateMSBuildProject(project, options, version, generator_flags) 
 942    else: 
 943      return _GenerateMSVSProject(project, options, version, generator_flags) 

 944   
 945   
 946  # TODO: Avoid code duplication with _ValidateSourcesForOSX in make.py. 

 947 -def _ValidateSourcesForMSVSProject(spec, version): 

 948    """Makes sure if duplicate basenames are not specified in the source list. 
 949   
 950    Arguments: 
 951      spec: The target dictionary containing the properties of the target. 
 952      version: The VisualStudioVersion object. 
 953    """ 
 954    # This validation should not be applied to MSVC2010 and later. 
 955    assert not version.UsesVcxproj() 
 956   
 957    # TODO: Check if MSVC allows this for loadable_module targets. 
 958    if spec.get('type', None) not in ('static_library', 'shared_library'): 
 959      return 
 960    sources = spec.get('sources', []) 
 961    basenames = {} 
 962    for source in sources: 
 963      name, ext = os.path.splitext(source) 
 964      is_compiled_file = ext in [ 
 965          '.c', '.cc', '.cpp', '.cxx', '.m', '.mm', '.s', '.S'] 
 966      if not is_compiled_file: 
 967        continue 
 968      basename = os.path.basename(name)  # Don't include extension. 
 969      basenames.setdefault(basename, []).append(source) 
 970   
 971    error = '' 
 972    for basename, files in basenames.iteritems(): 
 973      if len(files) > 1: 
 974        error += '  %s: %s\n' % (basename, ' '.join(files)) 
 975   
 976    if error: 
 977      print('static library %s has several files with the same basename:\n' % 
 978            spec['target_name'] + error + 'MSVC08 cannot handle that.') 
 979      raise GypError('Duplicate basenames in sources section, see list above') 

 980   
 981   

 982 -def _GenerateMSVSProject(project, options, version, generator_flags): 

 983    """Generates a .vcproj file.  It may create .rules and .user files too. 
 984   
 985    Arguments: 
 986      project: The project object we will generate the file for. 
 987      options: Global options passed to the generator. 
 988      version: The VisualStudioVersion object. 
 989      generator_flags: dict of generator-specific flags. 
 990    """ 
 991    spec = project.spec 
 992    gyp.common.EnsureDirExists(project.path) 
 993   
 994    platforms = _GetUniquePlatforms(spec) 
 995    p = MSVSProject.Writer(project.path, version, spec['target_name'], 
 996                           project.guid, platforms) 
 997   
 998    # Get directory project file is in. 
 999    project_dir = os.path.split(project.path)[0] 
1000    gyp_path = _NormalizedSource(project.build_file) 
1001    relative_path_of_gyp_file = gyp.common.RelativePath(gyp_path, project_dir) 
1002   
1003    config_type = _GetMSVSConfigurationType(spec, project.build_file) 
1004    for config_name, config in spec['configurations'].iteritems(): 
1005      _AddConfigurationToMSVSProject(p, spec, config_type, config_name, config) 
1006   
1007    # MSVC08 and prior version cannot handle duplicate basenames in the same 
1008    # target. 
1009    # TODO: Take excluded sources into consideration if possible. 
1010    _ValidateSourcesForMSVSProject(spec, version) 
1011   
1012    # Prepare list of sources and excluded sources. 
1013    gyp_file = os.path.split(project.build_file)[1] 
1014    sources, excluded_sources = _PrepareListOfSources(spec, generator_flags, 
1015                                                      gyp_file) 
1016   
1017    # Add rules. 
1018    actions_to_add = {} 
1019    _GenerateRulesForMSVS(p, project_dir, options, spec, 
1020                          sources, excluded_sources, 
1021                          actions_to_add) 
1022    list_excluded = generator_flags.get('msvs_list_excluded_files', True) 
1023    sources, excluded_sources, excluded_idl = ( 
1024        _AdjustSourcesAndConvertToFilterHierarchy(spec, options, project_dir, 
1025                                                  sources, excluded_sources, 
1026                                                  list_excluded, version)) 
1027   
1028    # Add in files. 
1029    missing_sources = _VerifySourcesExist(sources, project_dir) 
1030    p.AddFiles(sources) 
1031   
1032    _AddToolFilesToMSVS(p, spec) 
1033    _HandlePreCompiledHeaders(p, sources, spec) 
1034    _AddActions(actions_to_add, spec, relative_path_of_gyp_file) 
1035    _AddCopies(actions_to_add, spec) 
1036    _WriteMSVSUserFile(project.path, version, spec) 
1037   
1038    # NOTE: this stanza must appear after all actions have been decided. 
1039    # Don't excluded sources with actions attached, or they won't run. 
1040    excluded_sources = _FilterActionsFromExcluded( 
1041        excluded_sources, actions_to_add) 
1042    _ExcludeFilesFromBeingBuilt(p, spec, excluded_sources, excluded_idl, 
1043                                list_excluded) 
1044    _AddAccumulatedActionsToMSVS(p, spec, actions_to_add) 
1045   
1046    # Write it out. 
1047    p.WriteIfChanged() 
1048   
1049    return missing_sources 

1050   
1051   

1052 -def _GetUniquePlatforms(spec): 

1053    """Returns the list of unique platforms for this spec, e.g ['win32', ...]. 
1054   
1055    Arguments: 
1056      spec: The target dictionary containing the properties of the target. 
1057    Returns: 
1058      The MSVSUserFile object created. 
1059    """ 
1060    # Gather list of unique platforms. 
1061    platforms = OrderedSet() 
1062    for configuration in spec['configurations']: 
1063      platforms.add(_ConfigPlatform(spec['configurations'][configuration])) 
1064    platforms = list(platforms) 
1065    return platforms 

1066   
1067   

1068 -def _CreateMSVSUserFile(proj_path, version, spec): 

1069    """Generates a .user file for the user running this Gyp program. 
1070   
1071    Arguments: 
1072      proj_path: The path of the project file being created.  The .user file 
1073                 shares the same path (with an appropriate suffix). 
1074      version: The VisualStudioVersion object. 
1075      spec: The target dictionary containing the properties of the target. 
1076    Returns: 
1077      The MSVSUserFile object created. 
1078    """ 
1079    (domain, username) = _GetDomainAndUserName() 
1080    vcuser_filename = '.'.join([proj_path, domain, username, 'user']) 
1081    user_file = MSVSUserFile.Writer(vcuser_filename, version, 
1082                                    spec['target_name']) 
1083    return user_file 

1084   
1085   

1086 -def _GetMSVSConfigurationType(spec, build_file): 

1087    """Returns the configuration type for this project. 
1088   
1089    It's a number defined by Microsoft.  May raise an exception. 
1090   
1091    Args: 
1092        spec: The target dictionary containing the properties of the target. 
1093        build_file: The path of the gyp file. 
1094    Returns: 
1095        An integer, the configuration type. 
1096    """ 
1097    try: 
1098      config_type = { 
1099          'executable': '1',  # .exe 
1100          'shared_library': '2',  # .dll 
1101          'loadable_module': '2',  # .dll 
1102          'static_library': '4',  # .lib 
1103          'none': '10',  # Utility type 
1104          }[spec['type']] 
1105    except KeyError: 
1106      if spec.get('type'): 
1107        raise GypError('Target type %s is not a valid target type for ' 
1108                       'target %s in %s.' % 
1109                       (spec['type'], spec['target_name'], build_file)) 
1110      else: 
1111        raise GypError('Missing type field for target %s in %s.' % 
1112                       (spec['target_name'], build_file)) 
1113    return config_type 

1114   
1115   

1116 -def _AddConfigurationToMSVSProject(p, spec, config_type, config_name, config): 

1117    """Adds a configuration to the MSVS project. 
1118   
1119    Many settings in a vcproj file are specific to a configuration.  This 
1120    function the main part of the vcproj file that's configuration specific. 
1121   
1122    Arguments: 
1123      p: The target project being generated. 
1124      spec: The target dictionary containing the properties of the target. 
1125      config_type: The configuration type, a number as defined by Microsoft. 
1126      config_name: The name of the configuration. 
1127      config: The dictionary that defines the special processing to be done 
1128              for this configuration. 
1129    """ 
1130    # Get the information for this configuration 
1131    include_dirs, midl_include_dirs, resource_include_dirs = \ 
1132        _GetIncludeDirs(config) 
1133    libraries = _GetLibraries(spec) 
1134    library_dirs = _GetLibraryDirs(config) 
1135    out_file, vc_tool, _ = _GetOutputFilePathAndTool(spec, msbuild=False) 
1136    defines = _GetDefines(config) 
1137    defines = [_EscapeCppDefineForMSVS(d) for d in defines] 
1138    disabled_warnings = _GetDisabledWarnings(config) 
1139    prebuild = config.get('msvs_prebuild') 
1140    postbuild = config.get('msvs_postbuild') 
1141    def_file = _GetModuleDefinition(spec) 
1142    precompiled_header = config.get('msvs_precompiled_header') 
1143   
1144    # Prepare the list of tools as a dictionary. 
1145    tools = dict() 
1146    # Add in user specified msvs_settings. 
1147    msvs_settings = config.get('msvs_settings', {}) 
1148    MSVSSettings.ValidateMSVSSettings(msvs_settings) 
1149   
1150    # Prevent default library inheritance from the environment. 
1151    _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCLinkerTool', 'AdditionalDependencies', ['$(NOINHERIT)']) 
1152   
1153    for tool in msvs_settings: 
1154      settings = config['msvs_settings'][tool] 
1155      for setting in settings: 
1156        _ToolAppend(tools, tool, setting, settings[setting]) 
1157    # Add the information to the appropriate tool 
1158    _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCCLCompilerTool', 
1159                'AdditionalIncludeDirectories', include_dirs) 
1160    _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCMIDLTool', 
1161                'AdditionalIncludeDirectories', midl_include_dirs) 
1162    _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCResourceCompilerTool', 
1163                'AdditionalIncludeDirectories', resource_include_dirs) 
1164    # Add in libraries. 
1165    _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCLinkerTool', 'AdditionalDependencies', libraries) 
1166    _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCLinkerTool', 'AdditionalLibraryDirectories', 
1167                library_dirs) 
1168    if out_file: 
1169      _ToolAppend(tools, vc_tool, 'OutputFile', out_file, only_if_unset=True) 
1170    # Add defines. 
1171    _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCCLCompilerTool', 'PreprocessorDefinitions', defines) 
1172    _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCResourceCompilerTool', 'PreprocessorDefinitions', 
1173                defines) 
1174    # Change program database directory to prevent collisions. 
1175    _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCCLCompilerTool', 'ProgramDataBaseFileName', 
1176                '$(IntDir)$(ProjectName)\\vc80.pdb', only_if_unset=True) 
1177    # Add disabled warnings. 
1178    _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCCLCompilerTool', 
1179                'DisableSpecificWarnings', disabled_warnings) 
1180    # Add Pre-build. 
1181    _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCPreBuildEventTool', 'CommandLine', prebuild) 
1182    # Add Post-build. 
1183    _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCPostBuildEventTool', 'CommandLine', postbuild) 
1184    # Turn on precompiled headers if appropriate. 
1185    if precompiled_header: 
1186      precompiled_header = os.path.split(precompiled_header)[1] 
1187      _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCCLCompilerTool', 'UsePrecompiledHeader', '2') 
1188      _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCCLCompilerTool', 
1189                  'PrecompiledHeaderThrough', precompiled_header) 
1190      _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCCLCompilerTool', 
1191                  'ForcedIncludeFiles', precompiled_header) 
1192    # Loadable modules don't generate import libraries; 
1193    # tell dependent projects to not expect one. 
1194    if spec['type'] == 'loadable_module': 
1195      _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCLinkerTool', 'IgnoreImportLibrary', 'true') 
1196    # Set the module definition file if any. 
1197    if def_file: 
1198      _ToolAppend(tools, 'VCLinkerTool', 'ModuleDefinitionFile', def_file) 
1199   
1200    _AddConfigurationToMSVS(p, spec, tools, config, config_type, config_name) 

1201   
1202   

1203 -def _GetIncludeDirs(config): 

1204    """Returns the list of directories to be used for #include directives. 
1205   
1206    Arguments: 
1207      config: The dictionary that defines the special processing to be done 
1208              for this configuration. 
1209    Returns: 
1210      The list of directory paths. 
1211    """ 
1212    # TODO(bradnelson): include_dirs should really be flexible enough not to 
1213    #                   require this sort of thing. 
1214    include_dirs = ( 
1215        config.get('include_dirs', []) + 
1216        config.get('msvs_system_include_dirs', [])) 
1217    midl_include_dirs = ( 
1218        config.get('midl_include_dirs', []) + 
1219        config.get('msvs_system_include_dirs', [])) 
1220    resource_include_dirs = config.get('resource_include_dirs', include_dirs) 
1221    include_dirs = _FixPaths(include_dirs) 
1222    midl_include_dirs = _FixPaths(midl_include_dirs) 
1223    resource_include_dirs = _FixPaths(resource_include_dirs) 
1224    return include_dirs, midl_include_dirs, resource_include_dirs 

1225   
1226   

1227 -def _GetLibraryDirs(config): 

1228    """Returns the list of directories to be used for library search paths. 
1229   
1230    Arguments: 
1231      config: The dictionary that defines the special processing to be done 
1232              for this configuration. 
1233    Returns: 
1234      The list of directory paths. 
1235    """ 
1236   
1237    library_dirs = config.get('library_dirs', []) 
1238    library_dirs = _FixPaths(library_dirs) 
1239    return library_dirs 

1240   
1241   

1242 -def _GetLibraries(spec): 

1243    """Returns the list of libraries for this configuration. 
1244   
1245    Arguments: 
1246      spec: The target dictionary containing the properties of the target. 
1247    Returns: 
1248      The list of directory paths. 
1249    """ 
1250    libraries = spec.get('libraries', []) 
1251    # Strip out -l, as it is not used on windows (but is needed so we can pass 
1252    # in libraries that are assumed to be in the default library path). 
1253    # Also remove duplicate entries, leaving only the last duplicate, while 
1254    # preserving order. 
1255    found = OrderedSet() 
1256    unique_libraries_list = [] 
1257    for entry in reversed(libraries): 
1258      library = re.sub(r'^\-l', '', entry) 
1259      if not os.path.splitext(library)[1]: 
1260        library += '.lib' 
1261      if library not in found: 
1262        found.add(library) 
1263        unique_libraries_list.append(library) 
1264    unique_libraries_list.reverse() 
1265    return unique_libraries_list 

1266   
1267   

1268 -def _GetOutputFilePathAndTool(spec, msbuild): 

1269    """Returns the path and tool to use for this target. 
1270   
1271    Figures out the path of the file this spec will create and the name of 
1272    the VC tool that will create it. 
1273   
1274    Arguments: 
1275      spec: The target dictionary containing the properties of the target. 
1276    Returns: 
1277      A triple of (file path, name of the vc tool, name of the msbuild tool) 
1278    """ 
1279    # Select a name for the output file. 
1280    out_file = '' 
1281    vc_tool = '' 
1282    msbuild_tool = '' 
1283    output_file_map = { 
1284        'executable': ('VCLinkerTool', 'Link', '$(OutDir)', '.exe'), 
1285        'shared_library': ('VCLinkerTool', 'Link', '$(OutDir)', '.dll'), 
1286        'loadable_module': ('VCLinkerTool', 'Link', '$(OutDir)', '.dll'), 
1287        'static_library': ('VCLibrarianTool', 'Lib', '$(OutDir)lib\\', '.lib'), 
1288    } 
1289    output_file_props = output_file_map.get(spec['type']) 
1290    if output_file_props and int(spec.get('msvs_auto_output_file', 1)): 
1291      vc_tool, msbuild_tool, out_dir, suffix = output_file_props 
1292      if spec.get('standalone_static_library', 0): 
1293        out_dir = '$(OutDir)' 
1294      out_dir = spec.get('product_dir', out_dir) 
1295      product_extension = spec.get('product_extension') 
1296      if product_extension: 
1297        suffix = '.' + product_extension 
1298      elif msbuild: 
1299        suffix = '$(TargetExt)' 
1300      prefix = spec.get('product_prefix', '') 
1301      product_name = spec.get('product_name', '$(ProjectName)') 
1302      out_file = ntpath.join(out_dir, prefix + product_name + suffix) 
1303    return out_file, vc_tool, msbuild_tool 

1304   
1305   

1306 -def _GetOutputTargetExt(spec): 

1307    """Returns the extension for this target, including the dot 
1308   
1309    If product_extension is specified, set target_extension to this to avoid 
1310    MSB8012, returns None otherwise. Ignores any target_extension settings in 
1311    the input files. 
1312   
1313    Arguments: 
1314      spec: The target dictionary containing the properties of the target. 
1315    Returns: 
1316      A string with the extension, or None 
1317    """ 
1318    target_extension = spec.get('product_extension') 
1319    if target_extension: 
1320      return '.' + target_extension 
1321    return None 

1322   
1323   

1324 -def _GetDefines(config): 

1325    """Returns the list of preprocessor definitions for this configuation. 
1326   
1327    Arguments: 
1328      config: The dictionary that defines the special processing to be done 
1329              for this configuration. 
1330    Returns: 
1331      The list of preprocessor definitions. 
1332    """ 
1333    defines = [] 
1334    for d in config.get('defines', []): 
1335      if type(d) == list: 
1336        fd = '='.join([str(dpart) for dpart in d]) 
1337      else: 
1338        fd = str(d) 
1339      defines.append(fd) 
1340    return defines 

1341   
1342   

1343 -def _GetDisabledWarnings(config): 

1344    return [str(i) for i in config.get('msvs_disabled_warnings', [])] 

1345   
1346   

1347 -def _GetModuleDefinition(spec): 

1348    def_file = '' 
1349    if spec['type'] in ['shared_library', 'loadable_module', 'executable']: 
1350      def_files = [s for s in spec.get('sources', []) if s.endswith('.def')] 
1351      if len(def_files) == 1: 
1352        def_file = _FixPath(def_files[0]) 
1353      elif def_files: 
1354        raise ValueError( 
1355            'Multiple module definition files in one target, target %s lists ' 
1356            'multiple .def files: %s' % ( 
1357                spec['target_name'], ' '.join(def_files))) 
1358    return def_file 

1359   
1360   

1361 -def _ConvertToolsToExpectedForm(tools): 

1362    """Convert tools to a form expected by Visual Studio. 
1363   
1364    Arguments: 
1365      tools: A dictionary of settings; the tool name is the key. 
1366    Returns: 
1367      A list of Tool objects. 
1368    """ 
1369    tool_list = [] 
1370    for tool, settings in tools.iteritems(): 
1371      # Collapse settings with lists. 
1372      settings_fixed = {} 
1373      for setting, value in settings.iteritems(): 
1374        if type(value) == list: 
1375          if ((tool == 'VCLinkerTool' and 
1376               setting == 'AdditionalDependencies') or 
1377              setting == 'AdditionalOptions'): 
1378            settings_fixed[setting] = ' '.join(value) 
1379          else: 
1380            settings_fixed[setting] = ';'.join(value) 
1381        else: 
1382          settings_fixed[setting] = value 
1383      # Add in this tool. 
1384      tool_list.append(MSVSProject.Tool(tool, settings_fixed)) 
1385    return tool_list 

1386   
1387   

1388 -def _AddConfigurationToMSVS(p, spec, tools, config, config_type, config_name): 

1389    """Add to the project file the configuration specified by config. 
1390   
1391    Arguments: 
1392      p: The target project being generated. 
1393      spec: the target project dict. 
1394      tools: A dictionary of settings; the tool name is the key. 
1395      config: The dictionary that defines the special processing to be done 
1396              for this configuration. 
1397      config_type: The configuration type, a number as defined by Microsoft. 
1398      config_name: The name of the configuration. 
1399    """ 
1400    attributes = _GetMSVSAttributes(spec, config, config_type) 
1401    # Add in this configuration. 
1402    tool_list = _ConvertToolsToExpectedForm(tools) 
1403    p.AddConfig(_ConfigFullName(config_name, config), 
1404                attrs=attributes, tools=tool_list) 

1405   
1406   

1407 -def _GetMSVSAttributes(spec, config, config_type): 

1408    # Prepare configuration attributes. 
1409    prepared_attrs = {} 
1410    source_attrs = config.get('msvs_configuration_attributes', {}) 
1411    for a in source_attrs: 
1412      prepared_attrs[a] = source_attrs[a] 
1413    # Add props files. 
1414    vsprops_dirs = config.get('msvs_props', []) 
1415    vsprops_dirs = _FixPaths(vsprops_dirs) 
1416    if vsprops_dirs: 
1417      prepared_attrs['InheritedPropertySheets'] = ';'.join(vsprops_dirs) 
1418    # Set configuration type. 
1419    prepared_attrs['ConfigurationType'] = config_type 
1420    output_dir = prepared_attrs.get('OutputDirectory', 
1421                                    '$(SolutionDir)$(ConfigurationName)') 
1422    prepared_attrs['OutputDirectory'] = _FixPath(output_dir) + '\\' 
1423    if 'IntermediateDirectory' not in prepared_attrs: 
1424      intermediate = '$(ConfigurationName)\\obj\\$(ProjectName)' 
1425      prepared_attrs['IntermediateDirectory'] = _FixPath(intermediate) + '\\' 
1426    else: 
1427      intermediate = _FixPath(prepared_attrs['IntermediateDirectory']) + '\\' 
1428      intermediate = MSVSSettings.FixVCMacroSlashes(intermediate) 
1429      prepared_attrs['IntermediateDirectory'] = intermediate 
1430    return prepared_attrs 

1431   
1432   

1433 -def _AddNormalizedSources(sources_set, sources_array): 

1434    sources_set.update(_NormalizedSource(s) for s in sources_array) 

1435   
1436   

1437 -def _PrepareListOfSources(spec, generator_flags, gyp_file): 

1438    """Prepare list of sources and excluded sources. 
1439   
1440    Besides the sources specified directly in the spec, adds the gyp file so 
1441    that a change to it will cause a re-compile. Also adds appropriate sources 
1442    for actions and copies. Assumes later stage will un-exclude files which 
1443    have custom build steps attached. 
1444   
1445    Arguments: 
1446      spec: The target dictionary containing the properties of the target. 
1447      gyp_file: The name of the gyp file. 
1448    Returns: 
1449      A pair of (list of sources, list of excluded sources). 
1450      The sources will be relative to the gyp file. 
1451    """ 
1452    sources = OrderedSet() 
1453    _AddNormalizedSources(sources, spec.get('sources', [])) 
1454    excluded_sources = OrderedSet() 
1455    # Add in the gyp file. 
1456    if not generator_flags.get('standalone'): 
1457      sources.add(gyp_file) 
1458   
1459    # Add in 'action' inputs and outputs. 
1460    for a in spec.get('actions', []): 
1461      inputs = a['inputs'] 
1462      inputs = [_NormalizedSource(i) for i in inputs] 
1463      # Add all inputs to sources and excluded sources. 
1464      inputs = OrderedSet(inputs) 
1465      sources.update(inputs) 
1466      if not spec.get('msvs_external_builder'): 
1467        excluded_sources.update(inputs) 
1468      if int(a.get('process_outputs_as_sources', False)): 
1469        _AddNormalizedSources(sources, a.get('outputs', [])) 
1470    # Add in 'copies' inputs and outputs. 
1471    for cpy in spec.get('copies', []): 
1472      _AddNormalizedSources(sources, cpy.get('files', [])) 
1473    return (sources, excluded_sources) 

1474   
1475   

1476 -def _AdjustSourcesAndConvertToFilterHierarchy( 
1477      spec, options, gyp_dir, sources, excluded_sources, list_excluded, version): 

1478    """Adjusts the list of sources and excluded sources. 
1479   
1480    Also converts the sets to lists. 
1481   
1482    Arguments: 
1483      spec: The target dictionary containing the properties of the target. 
1484      options: Global generator options. 
1485      gyp_dir: The path to the gyp file being processed. 
1486      sources: A set of sources to be included for this project. 
1487      excluded_sources: A set of sources to be excluded for this project. 
1488      version: A MSVSVersion object. 
1489    Returns: 
1490      A trio of (list of sources, list of excluded sources, 
1491                 path of excluded IDL file) 
1492    """ 
1493    # Exclude excluded sources coming into the generator. 
1494    excluded_sources.update(OrderedSet(spec.get('sources_excluded', []))) 
1495    # Add excluded sources into sources for good measure. 
1496    sources.update(excluded_sources) 
1497    # Convert to proper windows form. 
1498    # NOTE: sources goes from being a set to a list here. 
1499    # NOTE: excluded_sources goes from being a set to a list here. 
1500    sources = _FixPaths(sources) 
1501    # Convert to proper windows form. 
1502    excluded_sources = _FixPaths(excluded_sources) 
1503   
1504    excluded_idl = _IdlFilesHandledNonNatively(spec, sources) 
1505   
1506    precompiled_related = _GetPrecompileRelatedFiles(spec) 
1507    # Find the excluded ones, minus the precompiled header related ones. 
1508    fully_excluded = [i for i in excluded_sources if i not in precompiled_related] 
1509   
1510    # Convert to folders and the right slashes. 
1511    sources = [i.split('\\') for i in sources] 
1512    sources = _ConvertSourcesToFilterHierarchy(sources, excluded=fully_excluded, 
1513                                               list_excluded=list_excluded, 
1514                                               msvs_version=version) 
1515   
1516    # Prune filters with a single child to flatten ugly directory structures 
1517    # such as ../../src/modules/module1 etc. 
1518    if version.UsesVcxproj(): 
1519      while all([isinstance(s, MSVSProject.Filter) for s in sources]) \ 
1520          and len(set([s.name for s in sources])) == 1: 
1521        assert all([len(s.contents) == 1 for s in sources]) 
1522        sources = [s.contents[0] for s in sources] 
1523    else: 
1524      while len(sources) == 1 and isinstance(sources[0], MSVSProject.Filter): 
1525        sources = sources[0].contents 
1526   
1527    return sources, excluded_sources, excluded_idl 

1528   
1529   

1530 -def _IdlFilesHandledNonNatively(spec, sources): 

1531    # If any non-native rules use 'idl' as an extension exclude idl files. 
1532    # Gather a list here to use later. 
1533    using_idl = False 
1534    for rule in spec.get('rules', []): 
1535      if rule['extension'] == 'idl' and int(rule.get('msvs_external_rule', 0)): 
1536        using_idl = True 
1537        break 
1538    if using_idl: 
1539      excluded_idl = [i for i in sources if i.endswith('.idl')] 
1540    else: 
1541      excluded_idl = [] 
1542    return excluded_idl 

1543   
1544   

1545 -def _GetPrecompileRelatedFiles(spec): 

1546    # Gather a list of precompiled header related sources. 
1547    precompiled_related = [] 
1548    for _, config in spec['configurations'].iteritems(): 
1549      for k in precomp_keys: 
1550        f = config.get(k) 
1551        if f: 
1552          precompiled_related.append(_FixPath(f)) 
1553    return precompiled_related 

1554   
1555   

1556 -def _ExcludeFilesFromBeingBuilt(p, spec, excluded_sources, excluded_idl, 
1557                                  list_excluded): 

1558    exclusions = _GetExcludedFilesFromBuild(spec, excluded_sources, excluded_idl) 
1559    for file_name, excluded_configs in exclusions.iteritems(): 
1560      if (not list_excluded and 
1561              len(excluded_configs) == len(spec['configurations'])): 
1562        # If we're not listing excluded files, then they won't appear in the 
1563        # project, so don't try to configure them to be excluded. 
1564        pass 
1565      else: 
1566        for config_name, config in excluded_configs: 
1567          p.AddFileConfig(file_name, _ConfigFullName(config_name, config), 
1568                          {'ExcludedFromBuild': 'true'}) 

1569   
1570   

1571 -def _GetExcludedFilesFromBuild(spec, excluded_sources, excluded_idl): 

1572    exclusions = {} 
1573    # Exclude excluded sources from being built. 
1574    for f in excluded_sources: 
1575      excluded_configs = [] 
1576      for config_name, config in spec['configurations'].iteritems(): 
1577        precomped = [_FixPath(config.get(i, '')) for i in precomp_keys] 
1578        # Don't do this for ones that are precompiled header related. 
1579        if f not in precomped: 
1580          excluded_configs.append((config_name, config)) 
1581      exclusions[f] = excluded_configs 
1582    # If any non-native rules use 'idl' as an extension exclude idl files. 
1583    # Exclude them now. 
1584    for f in excluded_idl: 
1585      excluded_configs = [] 
1586      for config_name, config in spec['configurations'].iteritems(): 
1587        excluded_configs.append((config_name, config)) 
1588      exclusions[f] = excluded_configs 
1589    return exclusions 

1590   
1591   

1592 -def _AddToolFilesToMSVS(p, spec): 

1593    # Add in tool files (rules). 
1594    tool_files = OrderedSet() 
1595    for _, config in spec['configurations'].iteritems(): 
1596      for f in config.get('msvs_tool_files', []): 
1597        tool_files.add(f) 
1598    for f in tool_files: 
1599      p.AddToolFile(f) 

1600   
1601   

1602 -def _HandlePreCompiledHeaders(p, sources, spec): 

1603    # Pre-compiled header source stubs need a different compiler flag 
1604    # (generate precompiled header) and any source file not of the same 
1605    # kind (i.e. C vs. C++) as the precompiled header source stub needs 
1606    # to have use of precompiled headers disabled. 
1607    extensions_excluded_from_precompile = [] 
1608    for config_name, config in spec['configurations'].iteritems(): 
1609      source = config.get('msvs_precompiled_source') 
1610      if source: 
1611        source = _FixPath(source) 
1612        # UsePrecompiledHeader=1 for if using precompiled headers. 
1613        tool = MSVSProject.Tool('VCCLCompilerTool', 
1614                                {'UsePrecompiledHeader': '1'}) 
1615        p.AddFileConfig(source, _ConfigFullName(config_name, config), 
1616                        {}, tools=[tool]) 
1617        basename, extension = os.path.splitext(source) 
1618        if extension == '.c': 
1619          extensions_excluded_from_precompile = ['.cc', '.cpp', '.cxx'] 
1620        else: 
1621          extensions_excluded_from_precompile = ['.c'] 
1622    def DisableForSourceTree(source_tree): 
1623      for source in source_tree: 
1624        if isinstance(source, MSVSProject.Filter): 
1625          DisableForSourceTree(source.contents) 
1626        else: 
1627          basename, extension = os.path.splitext(source) 
1628          if extension in extensions_excluded_from_precompile: 
1629            for config_name, config in spec['configurations'].iteritems(): 
1630              tool = MSVSProject.Tool('VCCLCompilerTool', 
1631                                      {'UsePrecompiledHeader': '0', 
1632                                       'ForcedIncludeFiles': '$(NOINHERIT)'}) 
1633              p.AddFileConfig(_FixPath(source), 
1634                              _ConfigFullName(config_name, config), 
1635                              {}, tools=[tool]) 

1636    # Do nothing if there was no precompiled source. 
1637    if extensions_excluded_from_precompile: 
1638      DisableForSourceTree(sources) 
1639   
1640   

1641 -def _AddActions(actions_to_add, spec, relative_path_of_gyp_file): 

1642    # Add actions. 
1643    actions = spec.get('actions', []) 
1644    # Don't setup_env every time. When all the actions are run together in one 
1645    # batch file in VS, the PATH will grow too long. 
1646    # Membership in this set means that the cygwin environment has been set up, 
1647    # and does not need to be set up again. 
1648    have_setup_env = set() 
1649    for a in actions: 
1650      # Attach actions to the gyp file if nothing else is there. 
1651      inputs = a.get('inputs') or [relative_path_of_gyp_file] 
1652      attached_to = inputs[0] 
1653      need_setup_env = attached_to not in have_setup_env 
1654      cmd = _BuildCommandLineForRule(spec, a, has_input_path=False, 
1655                                     do_setup_env=need_setup_env) 
1656      have_setup_env.add(attached_to) 
1657      # Add the action. 
1658      _AddActionStep(actions_to_add, 
1659                     inputs=inputs, 
1660                     outputs=a.get('outputs', []), 
1661                     description=a.get('message', a['action_name']), 
1662                     command=cmd) 

1663   
1664   

1665 -def _WriteMSVSUserFile(project_path, version, spec): 

1666    # Add run_as and test targets. 
1667    if 'run_as' in spec: 
1668      run_as = spec['run_as'] 
1669      action = run_as.get('action', []) 
1670      environment = run_as.get('environment', []) 
1671      working_directory = run_as.get('working_directory', '.') 
1672    elif int(spec.get('test', 0)): 
1673      action = ['$(TargetPath)', '--gtest_print_time'] 
1674      environment = [] 
1675      working_directory = '.' 
1676    else: 
1677      return  # Nothing to add 
1678    # Write out the user file. 
1679    user_file = _CreateMSVSUserFile(project_path, version, spec) 
1680    for config_name, c_data in spec['configurations'].iteritems(): 
1681      user_file.AddDebugSettings(_ConfigFullName(config_name, c_data), 
1682                                 action, environment, working_directory) 
1683    user_file.WriteIfChanged() 

1684   
1685   

1686 -def _AddCopies(actions_to_add, spec): 

1687    copies = _GetCopies(spec) 
1688    for inputs, outputs, cmd, description in copies: 
1689      _AddActionStep(actions_to_add, inputs=inputs, outputs=outputs, 
1690                     description=description, command=cmd) 

1691   
1692   

1693 -def _GetCopies(spec): 

1694    copies = [] 
1695    # Add copies. 
1696    for cpy in spec.get('copies', []): 
1697      for src in cpy.get('files', []): 
1698        dst = os.path.join(cpy['destination'], os.path.basename(src)) 
1699        # _AddCustomBuildToolForMSVS() will call _FixPath() on the inputs and 
1700        # outputs, so do the same for our generated command line. 
1701        if src.endswith('/'): 
1702          src_bare = src[:-1] 
1703          base_dir = posixpath.split(src_bare)[0] 
1704          outer_dir = posixpath.split(src_bare)[1] 
1705          cmd = 'cd "%s" && xcopy /e /f /y "%s" "%s\\%s\\"' % ( 
1706              _FixPath(base_dir), outer_dir, _FixPath(dst), outer_dir) 
1707          copies.append(([src], ['dummy_copies', dst], cmd, 
1708                         'Copying %s to %s' % (src, dst))) 
1709        else: 
1710          cmd = 'mkdir "%s" 2>nul & set ERRORLEVEL=0 & copy /Y "%s" "%s"' % ( 
1711              _FixPath(cpy['destination']), _FixPath(src), _FixPath(dst)) 
1712          copies.append(([src], [dst], cmd, 'Copying %s to %s' % (src, dst))) 
1713    return copies 

1714   
1715   

1716 -def _GetPathDict(root, path): 

1717    # |path| will eventually be empty (in the recursive calls) if it was initially 
1718    # relative; otherwise it will eventually end up as '\', 'D:\', etc. 
1719    if not path or path.endswith(os.sep): 
1720      return root 
1721    parent, folder = os.path.split(path) 
1722    parent_dict = _GetPathDict(root, parent) 
1723    if folder not in parent_dict: 
1724      parent_dict[folder] = dict() 
1725    return parent_dict[folder] 

1726   
1727   

1728 -def _DictsToFolders(base_path, bucket, flat): 

1729    # Convert to folders recursively. 
1730    children = [] 
1731    for folder, contents in bucket.iteritems(): 
1732      if type(contents) == dict: 
1733        folder_children = _DictsToFolders(os.path.join(base_path, folder), 
1734                                          contents, flat) 
1735        if flat: 
1736          children += folder_children 
1737        else: 
1738          folder_children = MSVSNew.MSVSFolder(os.path.join(base_path, folder), 
1739                                               name='(' + folder + ')', 
1740                                               entries=folder_children) 
1741          children.append(folder_children) 
1742      else: 
1743        children.append(contents) 
1744    return children 

1745   
1746   

1747 -def _CollapseSingles(parent, node): 

1748    # Recursively explorer the tree of dicts looking for projects which are 
1749    # the sole item in a folder which has the same name as the project. Bring 
1750    # such projects up one level. 
1751    if (type(node) == dict and 
1752        len(node) == 1 and 
1753        node.keys()[0] == parent + '.vcproj'): 
1754      return node[node.keys()[0]] 
1755    if type(node) != dict: 
1756      return node 
1757    for child in node: 
1758      node[child] = _CollapseSingles(child, node[child]) 
1759    return node 

1760   
1761   

1762 -def _GatherSolutionFolders(sln_projects, project_objects, flat): 

1763    root = {} 
1764    # Convert into a tree of dicts on path. 
1765    for p in sln_projects: 
1766      gyp_file, target = gyp.common.ParseQualifiedTarget(p)[0:2] 
1767      gyp_dir = os.path.dirname(gyp_file) 
1768      path_dict = _GetPathDict(root, gyp_dir) 
1769      path_dict[target + '.vcproj'] = project_objects[p] 
1770    # Walk down from the top until we hit a folder that has more than one entry. 
1771    # In practice, this strips the top-level "src/" dir from the hierarchy in 
1772    # the solution. 
1773    while len(root) == 1 and type(root[root.keys()[0]]) == dict: 
1774      root = root[root.keys()[0]] 
1775    # Collapse singles. 
1776    root = _CollapseSingles('', root) 
1777    # Merge buckets until everything is a root entry. 
1778    return _DictsToFolders('', root, flat) 

1779   
1780   

1781 -def _GetPathOfProject(qualified_target, spec, options, msvs_version): 

1782    default_config = _GetDefaultConfiguration(spec) 
1783    proj_filename = default_config.get('msvs_existing_vcproj') 
1784    if not proj_filename: 
1785      proj_filename = (spec['target_name'] + options.suffix + 
1786                       msvs_version.ProjectExtension()) 
1787   
1788    build_file = gyp.common.BuildFile(qualified_target) 
1789    proj_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(build_file), proj_filename) 
1790    fix_prefix = None 
1791    if options.generator_output: 
1792      project_dir_path = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(proj_path)) 
1793      proj_path = os.path.join(options.generator_output, proj_path) 
1794      fix_prefix = gyp.common.RelativePath(project_dir_path, 
1795                                           os.path.dirname(proj_path)) 
1796    return proj_path, fix_prefix 

1797   
1798   

1799 -def _GetPlatformOverridesOfProject(spec): 

1800    # Prepare a dict indicating which project configurations are used for which 
1801    # solution configurations for this target. 
1802    config_platform_overrides = {} 
1803    for config_name, c in spec['configurations'].iteritems(): 
1804      config_fullname = _ConfigFullName(config_name, c) 
1805      platform = c.get('msvs_target_platform', _ConfigPlatform(c)) 
1806      fixed_config_fullname = '%s|%s' % ( 
1807          _ConfigBaseName(config_name, _ConfigPlatform(c)), platform) 
1808      config_platform_overrides[config_fullname] = fixed_config_fullname 
1809    return config_platform_overrides 

1810   
1811   

1812 -def _CreateProjectObjects(target_list, target_dicts, options, msvs_version): 

1813    """Create a MSVSProject object for the targets found in target list. 
1814   
1815    Arguments: 
1816      target_list: the list of targets to generate project objects for. 
1817      target_dicts: the dictionary of specifications. 
1818      options: global generator options. 
1819      msvs_version: the MSVSVersion object. 
1820    Returns: 
1821      A set of created projects, keyed by target. 
1822    """ 
1823    global fixpath_prefix 
1824    # Generate each project. 
1825    projects = {} 
1826    for qualified_target in target_list: 
1827      spec = target_dicts[qualified_target] 
1828      if spec['toolset'] != 'target': 
1829        raise GypError( 
1830            'Multiple toolsets not supported in msvs build (target %s)' % 
1831            qualified_target) 
1832      proj_path, fixpath_prefix = _GetPathOfProject(qualified_target, spec, 
1833                                                    options, msvs_version) 
1834      guid = _GetGuidOfProject(proj_path, spec) 
1835      overrides = _GetPlatformOverridesOfProject(spec) 
1836      build_file = gyp.common.BuildFile(qualified_target) 
1837      # Create object for this project. 
1838      obj = MSVSNew.MSVSProject( 
1839          proj_path, 
1840          name=spec['target_name'], 
1841          guid=guid, 
1842          spec=spec, 
1843          build_file=build_file, 
1844          config_platform_overrides=overrides, 
1845          fixpath_prefix=fixpath_prefix) 
1846      # Set project toolset if any (MS build only) 
1847      if msvs_version.UsesVcxproj(): 
1848        obj.set_msbuild_toolset( 
1849            _GetMsbuildToolsetOfProject(proj_path, spec, msvs_version)) 
1850      projects[qualified_target] = obj 
1851    # Set all the dependencies, but not if we are using an external builder like 
1852    # ninja 
1853    for project in projects.values(): 
1854      if not project.spec.get('msvs_external_builder'): 
1855        deps = project.spec.get('dependencies', []) 
1856        deps = [projects[d] for d in deps] 
1857        project.set_dependencies(deps) 
1858    return projects 

1859   
1860   

1861 -def _InitNinjaFlavor(params, target_list, target_dicts): 

1862    """Initialize targets for the ninja flavor. 
1863   
1864    This sets up the necessary variables in the targets to generate msvs projects 
1865    that use ninja as an external builder. The variables in the spec are only set 
1866    if they have not been set. This allows individual specs to override the 
1867    default values initialized here. 
1868    Arguments: 
1869      params: Params provided to the generator. 
1870      target_list: List of target pairs: 'base/base.gyp:base'. 
1871      target_dicts: Dict of target properties keyed on target pair. 
1872    """ 
1873    for qualified_target in target_list: 
1874      spec = target_dicts[qualified_target] 
1875      if spec.get('msvs_external_builder'): 
1876        # The spec explicitly defined an external builder, so don't change it. 
1877        continue 
1878   
1879      path_to_ninja = spec.get('msvs_path_to_ninja', 'ninja.exe') 
1880
